label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the letter carefully and figure out what Carmen Rivera is writing to Disney about. The letter starts with Carmen describing a recent trip to the Magic Kingdom with her family, mentioning that an event made the visit "truly magical." Then, she recounts an incident when her daughter Gloria had a meltdown because she couldn't take her ice-cream on the Space Mountain ride. A staff member named Murphy helped Gloria by promising to hold onto the ice-cream and give it back after the ride. But when they got off the ride, Murphy had bought a new ice-cream, making it seem like she kept the original one. Carmen knows Murphy replaced it, but Gloria thought it was the same one. Carmen then says, "We know that ___ tried hard to make our visit special." The last sentence is a thank you: "Thanks so much for going above and beyond!" So, the purpose of the letter is to express gratitude for the staff member's kindness and the way the situation was handled. The options are: (A) order ice-creams (B) promise to visit again (C) express her thanks (D) give some suggestions Option A doesn't
